title: Finding Belief Geometries with Sparse Autoencoders
summary: Understanding the geometric structure of internal representations is a central goal of mechanistic interpretability. Prior work has shown that transformers trained on sequences generated by hidden Markov models encode probabilistic belief states as simplex-shaped geometries in their residual stream, with vertices corresponding to latent generative states. Whether large language models trained on naturalistic text develop analogous geometric representations remains an open question. We introduce a pipeline for discovering candidate simplex-structured subspaces in transformer representations,...

metadata.comment: Uses sparse autoencoders and simplex fitting to search for belief-state geometry in transformer representations.

title: Aligning Progress and Feasibility: A Neuro-Symbolic Dual Memory Framework for Long-Horizon LLM Agents
summary: Large language models (LLMs) have demonstrated strong potential in long-horizon decision-making tasks, such as embodied manipulation and web interaction. However, agents frequently struggle with endless trial-and-error loops or deviate from the main objective in complex environments. We attribute these failures to two fundamental errors: global Progress Drift and local Feasibility Violation. Existing methods typically attempt to address both issues simultaneously using a single paradigm. However, these two challenges are fundamentally distinct: the former relies on fuzzy semantic planning,...
tags: Memory Structures and Agent Memory Systems

metadata.comment: Decouples long-horizon agent memory into semantic progress memory and symbolic feasibility memory for storing and validating past experience.
metadata.primary_topic_label: Memory Structures and Agent Memory Systems

title: The Compression Gap: Why Discrete Tokenization Limits Vision-Language-Action Model Scaling
summary: Scaling Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models by upgrading the vision encoder is expected to improve downstream manipulation performance--as it does in vision-language modeling. We show that this expectation fails when actions are represented as discrete tokens, and explain why through an information-theoretic principle we call the Compression Gap: in any visuomotor pipeline, scaling behavior is governed by the location of the tightest information bottleneck. When actions are continuous (e.g., Diffusion Policy), the vision encoder is the binding constraint, and upgrading it directly improves...

metadata.comment: Argues discrete action tokenization creates the binding information bottleneck that blocks scaling gains in vision-language-action models.

title: Understanding Latent Diffusability via Fisher Geometry
summary: Diffusion models often degrade when trained in latent spaces (e.g., VAEs), yet the formal causes remain poorly understood. We quantify latent-space diffusability through the rate of change of the Minimum Mean Squared Error (MMSE) along the diffusion trajectory. Our framework decomposes this MMSE rate into contributions from Fisher Information (FI) and Fisher Information Rate (FIR). We demonstrate that while global isometry ensures FI alignment, FIR is governed by the encoder's local geometric properties. Our analysis explicitly decouples latent geometric distortion into three measurable pen...

metadata.comment: Explains latent diffusion failure through Fisher information geometry, decomposing diffusability loss into measurable geometric penalties.

title: Learning the Signature of Memorization in Autoregressive Language Models
summary: All prior membership inference attacks for fine-tuned language models use hand-crafted heuristics (e.g., loss thresholding, Min-K\%, reference calibration), each bounded by the designer's intuition. We introduce the first transferable learned attack, enabled by the observation that fine-tuning any model on any corpus yields unlimited labeled data, since membership is known by construction. This removes the shadow model bottleneck and brings membership inference into the deep learning era: learning what matters rather than designing it, with generalization through training diversity and scal...

metadata.comment: Learns a transferable classifier for memorization signatures that generalizes across Transformer, Mamba, RWKV, and recurrent architectures.

title: Prism: Policy Reuse via Interpretable Strategy Mapping in Reinforcement Learning
summary: We present PRISM (Policy Reuse via Interpretable Strategy Mapping), a framework that grounds reinforcement learning agents' decisions in discrete, causally validated concepts and uses those concepts as a zero-shot transfer interface between agents trained with different algorithms. PRISM clusters each agent's encoder features into $K$ concepts via K-means. Causal intervention establishes that these concepts directly drive - not merely correlate with - agent behavior: overriding concept assignments changes the selected action in 69.4% of interventions ($p = 8.6 \times 10^{-86}$, 2500 interve...
tags: World Models, Exploration, and Open-Ended Reinforcement Learning

metadata.comment: Uses causally validated discrete concepts as an interface for zero-shot policy reuse across independently trained RL agents.
metadata.primary_topic_label: World Models, Exploration, and Open-Ended Reinforcement Learning

title: Opal: Private Memory for Personal AI
summary: Personal AI systems increasingly retain long-term memory of user activity, including documents, emails, messages, meetings, and ambient recordings. Trusted hardware can keep this data private, but struggles to scale with a growing datastore. This pushes the data to external storage, which exposes retrieval access patterns that leak private information to the application provider. Oblivious RAM (ORAM) is a cryptographic primitive that can hide these patterns, but it requires a fixed access budget, precluding the query-dependent traversals that agentic memory systems rely on for accuracy. We...
tags: Memory Structures and Agent Memory Systems

metadata.comment: Designs an agent memory system that keeps retrieval access patterns private via enclave reasoning plus oblivious external storage.
metadata.primary_topic_label: Memory Structures and Agent Memory Systems

title: Robust Learning with Optimal Error
summary: The result appears to substantially revise optimal-error understanding in adversarial-noise learning by elevating randomized hypotheses from a technical detail to the key principle.

metadata.comment: Shows randomized hypotheses achieve optimal error rates under several adversarial noise models, improving classic deterministic limits.
metadata.spotlight_comment: The result appears to substantially revise optimal-error understanding in adversarial-noise learning by elevating randomized hypotheses from a technical detail to the key principle.

title: Gradient Boosting within a Single Attention Layer
summary: Reframing attention as intra-layer boosting is a distinctive architectural idea that could seed a broader family of iterative-correction attention mechanisms.

metadata.comment: Adds a second attention pass that attends to first-pass residual error, casting attention itself as an in-layer gradient boosting procedure.
metadata.spotlight_comment: Reframing attention as intra-layer boosting is a distinctive architectural idea that could seed a broader family of iterative-correction attention mechanisms.

title: SEDGE: Structural Extrapolated Data Generation
summary: The paper proposes a fairly novel structural-extrapolation formulation that could seed a new methodological direction beyond standard data augmentation.

metadata.comment: Provides identifiability conditions and practical algorithms for generating data under extrapolated structural interventions beyond the observed distribution.
metadata.spotlight_comment: The paper proposes a fairly novel structural-extrapolation formulation that could seed a new methodological direction beyond standard data augmentation.

title: Reflective Context Learning: Studying the Optimization Primitives of Context Space
summary: It frames context-space learning as a general optimization paradigm for agents, which could seed a broader new research direction beyond isolated prompting methods.
tags: Memory Structures and Agent Memory Systems

metadata.comment: Recasts context updating for agents as an optimization problem and systematically imports primitives like batching, replay, and variance reduction.
metadata.primary_topic_label: Memory Structures and Agent Memory Systems
metadata.spotlight_comment: It frames context-space learning as a general optimization paradigm for agents, which could seed a broader new research direction beyond isolated prompting methods.

title: AXELRAM: Quantize Once, Never Dequantize
summary: Broadly important today: Proposes direct attention-score computation from quantized KV-cache indices without dequantization, plus analysis of sign-pattern instability.

metadata.comment: Proposes direct attention-score computation from quantized KV-cache indices without dequantization, plus analysis of sign-pattern instability.
metadata.spotlight_comment: Broadly important today: Proposes direct attention-score computation from quantized KV-cache indices without dequantization, plus analysis of sign-pattern instability.

title: AdaHOP: Fast and Accurate Low-Precision Training via Outlier-Pattern-Aware Rotation
summary: Broadly important today: Finds layer- and operand-specific outlier patterns for low-precision training and adapts Hadamard rotation/outlier extraction accordingly.

metadata.comment: Finds layer- and operand-specific outlier patterns for low-precision training and adapts Hadamard rotation/outlier extraction accordingly.
metadata.spotlight_comment: Broadly important today: Finds layer- and operand-specific outlier patterns for low-precision training and adapts Hadamard rotation/outlier extraction accordingly.

title: Steerable but Not Decodable: Function Vectors Operate Beyond the Logit Lens
summary: Broadly important today: Shows function vectors can causally steer behavior even when task information is not decodable by the logit lens at any layer.

metadata.comment: Shows function vectors can causally steer behavior even when task information is not decodable by the logit lens at any layer.
metadata.spotlight_comment: Broadly important today: Shows function vectors can causally steer behavior even when task information is not decodable by the logit lens at any layer.

title: Product-Stability: Provable Convergence for Gradient Descent on the Edge of Stability
summary: Broadly important today: Provides a structural condition, product-stability, giving convergence guarantees for gradient descent in the edge-of-stability regime.

metadata.comment: Provides a structural condition, product-stability, giving convergence guarantees for gradient descent in the edge-of-stability regime.
metadata.spotlight_comment: Broadly important today: Provides a structural condition, product-stability, giving convergence guarantees for gradient descent in the edge-of-stability regime.

title: Hierarchical Planning with Latent World Models
summary: It is a strong broadly relevant world-model paper, especially because it pairs a clean hierarchical planning idea with real-robot zero-shot control results.
tags: World Models, Exploration, and Open-Ended Reinforcement Learning

metadata.comment: Multi-timescale latent world models with hierarchical planning to reduce long-horizon planning complexity.
metadata.primary_topic_label: World Models, Exploration, and Open-Ended Reinforcement Learning
metadata.spotlight_comment: It is a strong broadly relevant world-model paper, especially because it pairs a clean hierarchical planning idea with real-robot zero-shot control results.

title: FluxMoE: Decoupling Expert Residency for High-Performance MoE Serving
summary: Broadly important today: Introduces expert paging to decouple MoE expert residency from GPU memory, reallocating memory to KV cache for higher serving throughput.

metadata.comment: Introduces expert paging to decouple MoE expert residency from GPU memory, reallocating memory to KV cache for higher serving throughput.
metadata.spotlight_comment: Broadly important today: Introduces expert paging to decouple MoE expert residency from GPU memory, reallocating memory to KV cache for higher serving throughput.
